content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom indices labeled. Let me examine atom index 10 (C:10) more carefully.

From the image, I can see atom C:10 (carbon, index 10) is connected to:
- Atom C:9 (carbon, index 9) - visible as a direct bond
- Atom C:11 (carbon, index 11) - visible as a direct bond  
- Atom C:13 (carbon, index 13) - visible as a direct bond
- Atom H:67 (hydrogen, index 67) - visible as a direct bond

I missed the hydrogen atom in my previous assessment. Looking closely at atom C:10, it has four bonds total: three to carbon atoms (C:9, C:11, C:13) and one to hydrogen (H:67).

Let me confirm this is correct by checking if there are any other connections. The carbon atom at position 10 appears to be tetrahedrally bonded with these four atoms.

Based on the image, the neighbor atoms of atom index 10 (C) are:
- Atom 9 (C)
- Atom 11 (C)
- Atom 13 (C)
- Atom 67 (H)

Regarding the geomconf_index, from the file name "geomconf_4094.xyz", I can infer that the geomconf_index is 4094.

Let me provide the final answer:

The neighbor atoms of atom index 10 (C) are:
- Atom 9 (C)
- Atom 11 (C)
- Atom 13 (C)
- Atom 67 (H)

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 10, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
